human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for wealth-operations boundary discipline, source quality, custody and settlement evidence handling, fraud and client-protection escalation posture, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to wealth-operations execution work such as asset transfers, money movement, settlement blockers, reconciliation exceptions, and completion evidence capture.
Authority sources cover FINRA transfer and recordkeeping rules, SEC settlement and custody guidance, DTCC ACATS workflow references, and FinCEN or FINRA client-protection escalation guidance.
Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete artifacts, rework queues, completion-evidence refusal, realistic terminology, fraud or AML escalation, conflicting-policy handling, and adjacent-lane routing.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/financial-services/wealth-operations-specialist/ so wealth-operations work stays distinct from advisory, fraud, AML, treasury approval, and legal lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around approval overrides, advisory drift, legal interpretation, fraud and AML escalation, and completion without evidence.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated wealth-operations specialist that supports evidence-backed transfer, custody, settlement, reconciliation, and escalation workflows.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
